with_treadmill.name: Treadmill zone-2 walk or jog
with_treadmill.notes: Effort you could repeat weekly—breathing up, still able to speak in short phrases.
with_treadmill.detail: 3 min easy warm-up. Build to a steady pace (often ~5–7 km/h flat, or slower with 1–3% incline for comfort). Keep ribs stacked over pelvis, light hands on rails only if needed for balance.

with_treadmill.name: Goblet squat
with_treadmill.detail: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell at chest. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Brace core, sit hips down between hips until thighs are near parallel (or as deep as pain-free). Drive feet into floor to stand—knees track over toes.

with_treadmill.name: Dumbbell bench press
with_treadmill.detail: Shoulder blades pinched slightly down and together on the bench. Press over mid-chest; elbows ~45° from ribs at bottom. Stop 1–2 reps before form breaks—quality over max load.

with_treadmill.name: Lat pulldown (neutral or pronated)
with_treadmill.detail: Thighs fixed under pad. Start with slight lean from tall torso, pull bar to upper chest by driving elbows toward hips. Control the return—full stretch at top without shrugging shoulders to ears.

with_treadmill.name: Romanian deadlift (DB or bar)
with_treadmill.detail: Soft knees, hinge at hips pushing butt back while bar or DBs stay close to legs. Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch—not when lower back rounds. Squeeze glutes to finish tall; avoid hyperextending spine at top.

with_treadmill.name: Half-kneeling single-arm cable press
with_treadmill.detail: Knee down under cable stack, glutes squeezed on rear leg. Press handle forward without torso twisting—ribs down. Full extension without shrugging; control stack on return.

with_treadmill.name: Face pull or band pull-apart
with_treadmill.targetMuscles: Rear delts, mid traps, external rotators
with_treadmill.detail: Elbows high, pull rope toward face separating hands at end range—think ‘thumbs pointing behind you.’ No leaning back; small weight, high quality reps for shoulder health.

with_treadmill.name: Dead bug or side plank
with_treadmill.detail: Dead bug: low back pressed to floor, slow opposite arm/leg extension without arching. Side plank: elbow under shoulder, hips stacked, squeeze glutes—modify with knees bent if needed.

with_cycle.name: Stationary bike steady ride
with_cycle.notes: Moderate resistance; smooth cadence ~70–90 RPM typical.
with_cycle.detail: 5 min gradual warm-up. Set resistance so legs work but you can maintain rhythm and nasal or controlled mouth breathing. Every 8–10 min add 10–20s slightly harder ‘push’ then settle back.

with_cycle.name: Leg press
with_cycle.detail: Feet mid-platform, hip-width. Lower until thighs approach parallel without pelvis rounding off pad. Press without locking knees violently; keep even pressure through full foot.

with_cycle.name: Seated cable row
with_cycle.detail: Neutral spine, slight lean from hips OK. Pull handle to lower ribs/sternum, squeeze shoulder blades at end. Return with protraction under control—no jerking torso.

with_cycle.name: Machine or DB shoulder press
with_cycle.detail: Brace core; press mostly vertical so wrists finish over shoulders not in front of face. Full range without bouncing—stop if lower back arches excessively (reduce weight or use back support).

with_cycle.name: Lying leg curl
with_cycle.detail: Align knee joint with machine axis. Control the negative—2–3 sec lowering. Avoid snapping or cutting range short; pause 1 sec at peak contraction.

with_cycle.name: Cable triceps pushdown (rope)
with_cycle.detail: Elbows pinned at sides, extend fully spreading rope ends at bottom without shoulders rolling forward. Upper arm stays vertical throughout.

with_cycle.name: Pallof press
with_cycle.detail: Stand perpendicular to cable, hands at sternum. Press arms straight out resisting rotation—hips and shoulders square. Slow return; exhale on press.

with_cycle.name: Calf raise (seated or standing)
with_cycle.detail: Full stretch at bottom, 1–2 sec pause at top. Standing bias gastroc; seated bias soleus—mix across weeks if both available.

without_cardio.name: Bodyweight or goblet squat
without_cardio.detail: Tempo 2–1–2 optional: controlled down, no bounce out of bottom. If knees cave, cue ‘spread the floor’ with feet. Add load only when depth and torso angle stay consistent.

without_cardio.name: Push-up (floor or incline)
without_cardio.detail: Hands under shoulders, body line from head to heels. Lower chest between hands; elbows ~30–45° from torso. Incline on bench/Smith bar if full floor reps aren’t clean yet.

without_cardio.name: Single-arm dumbbell row
without_cardio.detail: Flat back, hand on bench. Row toward hip pocket, not straight up—elbow tracks back. Minimize torso rotation; let the arm do the work.

without_cardio.name: Romanian deadlift
without_cardio.detail: Same hinge rules as treadmill variant—bar or DBs close, neutral spine, feel hamstrings load before standing tall with glutes (not hyperextending low back).

without_cardio.name: Walking lunge
without_cardio.detail: Short to medium steps to keep front knee over ankle-ish, not crashing past toes. Torso tall; use DBs at sides if stable. Stop set if knee pain—try reverse lunge in place instead.

without_cardio.name: Plank (forearm or high)
without_cardio.detail: Elbows under shoulders, squeeze glutes and quads, ribs down—no sagging hips or piked butt. Breathe shallow into sides, not holding breath entire hold.

without_cardio.name: Farmer carry
without_cardio.detail: Heavy but walkable DBs or kettlebells. Tall posture, short quick steps. Put weights down with hinge, not rounded back.

without_cardio.name: Side plank
without_cardio.detail: Elbow under shoulder, feet stacked or staggered for stability. Lift hips in one line; breathe. Bottom knee down modification is fine.

with_treadmill.name: Incline treadmill walk
with_treadmill.notes: Higher incline often lets you burn more at a walkable speed vs flat running for many people.
with_treadmill.detail: Warm-up 5 min flat. Incline 6–12% (start lower if new); brisk walk 3.5–5.5 km/h typical. Posture tall—don’t lean heavily on handles (light touch OK). Cool down 3–5 min lowering incline gradually.
alternatives: Hill intervals 1 min up / 1 min down

with_treadmill.name: Walking lunges or reverse lunges
with_treadmill.detail: Control the descent—knee tracks over foot. Add DBs only if balance is solid. Reverse lunges in place are easier on knees for some athletes.
alternatives: Sled push if gym has turf

with_treadmill.name: Kettlebell swing
with_treadmill.detail: Hinge pattern—not a squat. Power from hip snap at top; arms are ropes. Stop before lower back takes over (reduce reps or weight). Russian swing to eye level is fine for most gyms.

with_treadmill.name: Cable row or seated row
with_treadmill.detail: Full stretch, strong pull without torso throw. Pair with metabolic work this session—keep rows crisp, not sloppy for ‘more burn’.

with_treadmill.name: Goblet squat (continuous tension)
with_treadmill.detail: Moderate weight, smooth reps without locking out hard at top each rep if you want more time under tension—or full lock every rep if preferred. Keep elbows inside knees at bottom to open hips.

with_treadmill.name: Push-up or DB floor press
with_treadmill.detail: Metabolic-friendly: shorter rest acceptable if form stays strict. Stop 2 reps shy of breakdown to protect shoulders.

with_treadmill.name: Mountain climbers
with_treadmill.detail: Hands under shoulders, hips low-ish like plank start. Drive knees toward chest rhythmically without piking hips to ceiling. Slow tempo if low back feels it.

with_treadmill.name: Finisher: treadmill walk-out
with_treadmill.detail: After lifting, easy flat walk to bring heart rate down gradually—supports recovery vs stopping cold after hard intervals.

with_cycle.name: Bike intervals (HIIT-style)
with_cycle.notes: Hard = strong resistance + fast cadence you can still control safely.
with_cycle.detail: 10 min warm-up progressive. During ‘hard’: stay seated unless you’re experienced standing climbs. ‘Easy’ is active recovery—not zero resistance. Cool down 5 min spin easy.

with_cycle.name: Step-ups (controlled)
with_cycle.detail: Use bench or box at or slightly below knee height. Drive through heel/top foot; avoid pushing off bottom leg. Control down—don’t crash.

with_cycle.name: Dumbbell thruster
with_cycle.detail: Front squat depth with DBs at shoulders, then drive up into overhead press one fluid motion. Light–moderate load; breathe: exhale on press portion.
alternatives: Squat to push press barbell light

with_cycle.name: TRX or ring row
with_cycle.detail: Body straight like plank, pull chest to handles. Walk feet in for harder angle. Squeeze shoulder blades; don’t hip-hinge cheat.

with_cycle.name: Leg press (tempo)
with_cycle.detail: 3-1-2 tempo optional: 3 sec lower, 1 sec pause bottom, 2 sec up. Huge metabolic leg pump without jumping—joint-friendly for many.

with_cycle.name: Battle rope waves (alternating)
with_cycle.detail: Athletic stance, slight hinge. Move ropes with whole body, not just arms. Stop if shoulders burn sloppy—quality waves.

with_cycle.name: Bicycle crunch or dead bug
with_cycle.detail: Slow bicycle: elbow meets opposite knee, extend fully other side without jerking neck. Hands support head, don’t pull neck forward.

with_cycle.name: Finisher: steady spin
with_cycle.detail: Low resistance, nasal breathing if possible—closes session and aids recovery.

without_cardio.name: Jump rope (or phantom if no rope)
without_cardio.detail: Small jumps, wrists spin rope. Land softly on forefoot. High knees or boxer skip for variety. Mat if indoors to protect joints.

without_cardio.name: Burpee (step-back version)
without_cardio.detail: Hands down, step back to plank (not flop), optional push-up, step feet in, stand or small jump. Safer on wrists and low back than explosive chest-to-floor for many.

without_cardio.name: Squat to dumbbell press
without_cardio.detail: DBs at shoulders, squat deep enough for you, drive up and press overhead in one motion. Light weight for crisp reps.

without_cardio.name: Battle rope or slam ball circuit
without_cardio.detail: Alternate waves with slams every other round if equipment allows. Keep ribs down during slams—power from hips.

without_cardio.name: Kettlebell or DB swing
without_cardio.detail: Same hinge coaching as maintain block—this is a power cardio hybrid. Stop set if grip or back fails first.

without_cardio.name: Renegade row
without_cardio.detail: Plank on DBs, feet wide for base. Row one DB without rotating hips—widen stance more if needed. Light–moderate load.

without_cardio.name: Walking lunge finisher
without_cardio.detail: Bodyweight or light DBs. Last block of session—form over speed.

without_cardio.name: Russian twist (slow, weighted optional)
without_cardio.detail: Feet down or up for harder. Rotate through mid-back, not just flailing arms. Light plate or med ball; quality rotation.

with_treadmill.name: Treadmill easy warm-up
with_treadmill.notes: Keep cardio minimal before strength—goal is readiness, not fatigue.
with_treadmill.detail: Walk or very light jog. Finish with dynamic leg swings or bodyweight squats if you like—save energy for heavy compounds.

with_treadmill.name: Back squat (or safety bar)
with_treadmill.detail: Walk out tight, brace 360° breath, sit between hips. Depth you own with neutral spine—competition depth not required for hypertrophy if range is consistent week to week. Drive mid-foot.

with_treadmill.name: Barbell bench press
with_treadmill.detail: Leg drive, arch is personal/safe range, bar path slightly curved toward face off chest then up. Pause 1 sec on chest optional for control. Spotter or safety arms for heavy sets.

with_treadmill.name: Barbell row (pendlay or bent-over)
with_treadmill.detail: Hinge to ~45–90° torso, pull to lower ribs, squeeze back. Don’t use excessive body English on strict hypertrophy weeks—controlled cheat vs strength phase is a choice.

with_treadmill.name: Romanian deadlift
with_treadmill.detail: Heavier than fat-loss variant but same hinge integrity—if low back rounds, reduce load. Add 2.5–5 lb when all sets hit top of rep range with 1 RIR.

with_treadmill.name: Overhead press (standing or seated)
with_treadmill.detail: Glutes squeezed, ribs down. Bar path vertical or slight back arc for strict press. Full lockout without craning neck forward.

with_treadmill.name: Hanging leg raise or cable crunch
with_treadmill.detail: Leg raise: posterior pelvic tilt to initiate—avoid pure hip flexor swing. Cable crunch: curl spine, not just arms moving.

with_treadmill.name: Optional calf / arm accessories
with_treadmill.detail: Hypertrophy benefits from weekly volume per muscle—use 2–3 isolation moves if time allows after compounds. Standing calf + hammer curl example pairing.

with_cycle.name: Bike easy warm-up
with_cycle.detail: Low resistance, gradually raise cadence last 2 min. Not breathless before deadlifts.

with_cycle.name: Conventional or trap-bar deadlift
with_cycle.detail: Bar over mid-foot, hinge to grip, pull slack out, brace, drive floor away. Hips and chest rise together—no stiff-leg hips shooting first. Trap bar often allows more upright torso if mobility-limited.
alternatives: Rack pull from pins (partial ROM overload)

with_cycle.name: Incline dumbbell press
with_cycle.detail: Bench 15–30°. DB path slight arc converging at top without flaring elbows to 90°. Control eccentric 2–3 sec.

with_cycle.name: Weighted pull-up or lat pulldown
with_cycle.detail: Full hang to start each pull-up if possible; chest to bar or chin over based on shoulder comfort. Pulldown: lean slightly, pull to upper chest.

with_cycle.name: Bulgarian split squat
with_cycle.detail: Front foot far enough so knee tracks over ankle. Torso slight forward lean OK for glute bias. DBs at sides or goblet.

with_cycle.name: Farmer carry heavy
with_cycle.detail: Heavier than maintenance—handles should challenge grip last. Tall walk, short steps.

with_cycle.name: Lateral raise + rear delt superset
with_cycle.detail: Light to moderate—control, slight lean on laterals, thumbs slightly down finish cue optional. Rear delt fly bent-over or reverse pec deck.

with_cycle.name: Leg curl
with_cycle.detail: Hypertrophy: controlled eccentrics, full ROM. Pair hamstring volume with hinge patterns for knee health.

without_cardio.name: Leg press (heavy, full ROM)
without_cardio.detail: Feet placement changes bias—mid foot balanced. Depth without butt rounding off seat. No hands on knees pushing—hands on handles beside seat.

without_cardio.name: Dumbbell bench press
without_cardio.detail: Greater ROM than barbell for many—stretch at bottom without bouncing. Rotate neutral at bottom to slight pronated at top optional.

without_cardio.name: Chest-supported row (incline bench + DB)
without_cardio.detail: Chest on pad removes lower back limiter—row elbows back, squeeze scapula. Great for adding pulling volume safely.

without_cardio.name: Standing overhead press
without_cardio.detail: Squeeze glutes, quads; bar path vertical. Walk out from rack, 2–3 warm-up sets before working weights.

without_cardio.name: Hip thrust
without_cardio.detail: Upper back on bench, bar padded on hips. Chin tucked slightly, rib cage down, drive hips up with glutes max squeeze top—no hyperextension through lumbar only.

without_cardio.name: Cable crunch or weighted plank
without_cardio.detail: Progress load over weeks like any lift—add plate on cable stack when top rep range hit.
alternatives: Hanging knee raise with dumbbell between feet

without_cardio.name: EZ-bar curl + skull crusher superset
without_cardio.detail: Arm hypertrophy finisher—elbows fixed for extensions, no flaring on curls. Light elbow tendon-friendly tempo.

without_cardio.name: Calf raise heavy
without_cardio.detail: Pause stretch and contraction. Train calves 2×/week if lagging—higher weekly sets help growth (general hypertrophy principle).
